stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: more than 500 drones strike at Russia, killing 3; US allows Russian oil sanctions waiver to lapse
stories.summary: Large Ukrainian attack comes after Zelenskyy on Friday vowed retaliation for earlier Russian strikes. What we know on day 1,544 More than 500 Ukrainian drones attacked Russia overnight, killing three people around Moscow , authorities said on Sunday. Air defences shot down 556 drones in more than a dozen regions, including Moscow, Russia’s defence ministry said. The attack came after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy vowed on Friday to launch more retaliatory strikes because of an earlier

stories.title: The email glitch that blindsided Poland on US troop move
stories.summary: Poland was blindsided on Wednesday by the surprise U.S. decision to cancel a planned deployment of American troops to the country. But it turns out the warning had already arrived days earlier. Poland’s military was alerted to the Pentagon’s move as early as last Monday, according to three people with knowledge of the matter who are in the immediate circle of the Chief of the General Staff of the Polish Armed Forces, Gen. Wiesław Kukuła, who received the alert. But the message got stuck in the c

stories.title: US, China and Russia prefer a divided Europe, Kallas warns
stories.summary: TALLINN — The Trump administration doesn’t like a unified European Union because the bloc is a geopolitical power that Washington has to contend with, EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said on Sunday, urging EU member countries not to weaken the bloc by pursuing bilateral agreements with the U.S. “They don’t like the European Union, that’s very clear,” Kallas said at the Lennart Meri Conference in Tallinn on Sunday. “But we need to understand why they don’t like the EU; why China d

stories.title: Pope Leo launches AI commission
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV is creating a commission on artificial intelligence, a new Vatican body aimed at coordinating the Catholic Church’s response to AI. The move comes ahead of Pope Leo’s first encyclical, or pastoral letter, which is expected to prioritize an ethics-based approach to AI . The Vatican announced on Saturday that the pontiff had decided to launch the commission because of the increase in usage of AI, “its potential effects on human beings and on humanity as a whole [and] the church’s conc

stories.title: Could Romania and Moldova reunite?
stories.summary: Moldovan leader Maia Sandu and her Romanian counterpart Nicusor Dan want to reunite their countries. Is that a realistic scenario?

stories.title: „Nem tudtam, hogy a jó zsokénak előtte lónak kell lennie”
stories.summary: Május 30-án a sorozat történetében először rendezheti meg Budapest a Bajnokok Ligája döntőjét, a Puskás Arénában ünnepelhet Európa legjobb futballcsapata. A fináléig mindennap visszatekintünk a döntők egy-egy nagy pillanatára, egyéniségére, érdekességére.

stories.title: Az egyetlen, hivatali ideje alatt meggyilkolt brit miniszterelnök családi átka közel 200 év után teljesedett be
stories.summary: Egy magára hagyott kereskedő azt érezte, hogy senki nem segít neki, ezért 1812-ben megölte a miniszterelnököt. A britek odáig voltak érte, de később meglett ennek is a böjtje.

stories.title: Orbán Balázs megüzente, hogy álljak félre, mert ők lesznek a tulajdonosok
stories.summary: Így ejtette túszul a NER a könyvpiacot. Veszélybe került kiadói függetlenség, elfogadhatatlan vezetési kultúra és versenyjogi aggályok. Utánajártunk, hogyan növesztett lábat a NER a könyvpiacon, de arról is megkérdeztük a kiadók képviselőit, mit remélnek a kormányváltástól, és mik a terület megújulásának legfontosabb lépései.

stories.title: Veretlenül fejezte be a szezont a Benfica, Mourinho csapata így is csak harmadik lett
stories.summary: Nem először fordult ez elő a portugál sztárcsapattal a bajnokságban.

stories.title: Aranyozott plafon, bárpult a bírói klubban – fotókat mutatott be Magyar Péter a Kúria épületéről
stories.summary: A Kúria szombaton tagadta, hogy szivarszoba vagy éppen elnöki lakosztály szerepelt volna a felújítási tervekben.

stories.title: Ideje, hogy az egyház kipróbálja, milyen politikai nyomás nélkül létezni
stories.summary: Hogyan veszítették el a hitelüket magyar egyházi vezetők, miközben a Fidesz-KDNP alatt egyre közelebb sodródtak a politikához, de egyre távolabb a Bibliától? Skizofrén állapotba került a hazai kereszténység a saját tanítása és a politikai érdekek közötti szakadékban, és hogy fel tud-e ébredni a kómaszerű kábulatból a katolikus és református intézményrendszer, nehezen megválaszolható dilemma.

stories.title: Művésztelep 100 – megnyílt az év legfontosabb szentendrei kiállítása
stories.summary: Idén 100 éve, hogy néhány fiatal művész kapott egy használaton kívüli, kicsit lelakott, de hangulatos ingatlant Szentendrétől. Így született meg a szentendrei Művésztelep (mai nevén Régi Művésztelep), ahol Magyarországon egyedülálló módon azóta is folyamatosan alkotnak az aktuális tagok és vendégeik. A MűvészetMalomban nyílt Művésztelep 100 című jubileumi kiállításon mintegy 100 alkotó 220 kvalitásos festménye segítségével ismerjük meg ezt a történetet – amiről Bodonyi Emőke vezető kurátor beszé

stories.title: Tőzsdére készül Elon Musk űrhajós cége, a SpaceX, az értékét 7 évnyi magyar GDP magasságába becsülik
stories.summary: Már júniusban eljuthatnak a részvénykibocsátásig a tervek szerint.

stories.title: Ki fogja most bevenni Brüsszelt?
stories.summary: Folyamatosan tűnnek el a magyar utcákról az elmúlt 16 év tömegmanipulációs eszközeinek szimbólumai. A nem létező háborús fenyegetést közvetítő, százmilliárdokból legyártott kék plakátok lekerültek a falakról, de örök nyomot hagytak a társadalom pszichéjében. Körkép.

stories.title: Magyar Péter bejelentette, ki veszi át a helyét az Európai Parlamentben
stories.summary: Tarr Zoltán helyére is új képviselőt jelölt a Tisza.

stories.title: Egy nap alatt megérezhette az orosz nagykövet a kormányváltást Magyarországon
stories.summary: Szijjártó szövetséges országok nagyköveteit többször, az orosz nagykövetet viszont a háború kitörése óta sosem, előtte is csak ritkán kérette be külügyminisztersége 12 éve alatt. Utódja, Orbán Anita ezt hivatalba lépésének másnapján megtette a Kárpátalját ért orosz dróntámadás miatt. Ez önmagában jelzi az éles váltást a magyar diplomáciában.

stories.title: Fact-check: Tényleg nem érvényes Havasi Bertalan felmentése a Kabinetiroda átalakulása miatt?
stories.summary: Magyar Péter szombaton felmentette Havasi Bertalant, a Miniszterelnöki Kabinetiroda helyettes államtitkárát, amire több fideszes politikus azt reagálta: jogszerűtlen, mert a miniszterelnöknek azt Rogán Antalnak kellett volna javasolnia. Megnéztük, mit mondanak a jogszabályok.

stories.title: The Guardian view on life after Orbán: Péter Magyar’s fast start bodes well for Hungary and for Europe | Editorial
stories.summary: The new government in Budapest has already made an impact in Brussels. At home, the new prime minister is so far doing and saying the right things The transformative impact of Péter Magyar’s historic election victory over Viktor Orbán is already being felt in Brussels. On Monday, two days after Mr Magyar was sworn in as Hungary’s new prime minister, his new pro-EU government lifted the veto which for over a year has prevented the EU imposing sanctions on violent Israeli settlers. This followed a

stories.title: 13 men killed by US military boat strikes identified: ‘These were flesh-and-blood people’
stories.summary: All victims of US strikes in eastern Pacific and the Caribbean identified so far came from extremely poor communities Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email A five-month investigation has named 13 previously unidentified victims of US attacks on boats allegedly carrying narcotics in a campaign that has killed nearly 200 people in the Caribbean and the eastern Pacific. It is unclear if the US has ever identified any of its 194 victims before attacking them, and the names of just three

stories.title: I’m a Eurovision superfan, but this year’s contest brings only sadness. I won’t be tuning in
stories.summary: Mismanagement and political campaigning have sucked the joy out of an event meant to bring people together For the past two years, amid intensifying controversy over Israel’s participation in Eurovision, I and most other Eurovision superfans have stuck by the contest, despite clear misgivings. This week, however, as the usual collection of power ballads and jokey songs compete in Vienna, we are not bonding over a common joy, but rather over our shared sense of sadness about the politicisation of

stories.title: Nyert Kisvárdán, a Győri ETO lett az NB I. bajnoka, második a Fradi
stories.summary: A Ferencváros hiába nyert 3–0-ra a zárófordulóban, nem tudta megszerezni zsinórban nyolcadik bajnoki címét.

stories.title: Havasi Magyar Péternek: Hathavi összegről van szó, „amit, Öcsi, te az utolsó fillérig ki fogsz perkálni”
stories.summary: Nyilatkozott a Telexnek a felmentett helyettes államtitkár, aki levelében arról ír, hogy „nem mindig kormányoztunk jól”, „szarul kommunikáltunk”, „egyesek közülünk le is buktak”, de a politikai közösségük 99,95 százaléka tisztességes, becsületes ember.

stories.title: A Fradi edzője szerint a kormányzat részéről világos tájékoztatásra van szükség, hogy mi fog történni a magyar futballal
stories.summary: Robbie Keane elmondta, hogy vele még nem beszéltek a kormányváltásról, de megérti a szurkolók aggodalmát.

stories.title: Negyven éves minden idők legismertebb vadászgépes filmje, a Top Gun
stories.summary: A kritikusok lehúzták, üres látványosságnak, toborzó reklámfilmnek nevezték, mégis az év legnagyobb kasszasikere lett. Forradalmasította a hollywoodi filmkészítést, világsztárt csinált Tom Cruise-ból, és beindította a méltatlanul alulértékelt Tony Scott filmrendezői karrierjét.

stories.title: Roma children make history by performing Roma hymn at Hungarian parliament – video
stories.summary: Young, mostly Roma, members of the Sugo Tambura band realised their dream on Saturday by performing the Roma hymn at Hungary's parliament. The new prime minister, Péter Magyar, kept the promise he made to the children when he visited their village on the campaign trail. Roma rights campaigners have seized the moment, calling on the new government to ensure that the symbolism translates into real change Continue reading...

stories.title: Latvia: Prime Minister Evika Silina resigns over handling of drone incidents after coalition ally's withdrawal
stories.summary: Evika Silina says she is resigning her post, following the defense minister's resignation and the coalition government's collapse amid controversy over Ukrainian drones aimed at Russia that landed in the Baltic country.

stories.title: Tech founders use AI-generated images to poke fun at Anthony Albanese in protest against tax changes
stories.summary: ‘He’s having a great time with his new 47% equity,’ one entrepreneur jokes, warning that some startups may leave Australia behind Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Tech entrepreneurs have mocked the government’s capital gains tax changes by posting AI-generated photos of Anthony Albanese as their “new founder” and warning that increased taxes could push people away from working for new businesses or send startups overseas. Startups and entrepreneurs may yet receive a c

stories.title: At least four people killed in Russia as Ukraine launches retaliatory strikes
stories.summary: Wave of almost 600 drones launched across 14 regions, after Moscow’s deadly three-day attack on Ukraine last week At least four people have been killed in a large-scale retaliatory strike by Ukraine on Russia’s regions, including Moscow, the Russian authorities have said. The wave of almost 600 Ukrainian drones struck overnight across 14 Russian regions, as well as the Crimean peninsula and the Black and Azov seas, the Russian defence ministry said on Sunday, with the region around the capital a

stories.title: Trump-backed challenger defeats Republican senator who voted to convict president
stories.summary: Trump threw his full support behind Julia Letlow in Louisiana's high-stakes contest, branding the incumbent "disloyal".

stories.title: Large-scale Ukrainian drone attack kills three in Moscow region, says Russia
stories.summary: Ukraine's president says the strikes are a "justified" response to deadly Russian attacks on Ukrainian cities.

stories.title: ‘They may draw racist maps, but we are the south’: thousands rally in Alabama for Black voting rights
stories.summary: People came to Montgomery by bus, car and plane to march on the state capitol with local and national leaders Thousands of people from across the country descended on Montgomery, the capital of Alabama, on Saturday. They arrived by bus, by car and by plane to gather for the All Roads Lead to the South rally, following the supreme court’s Louisiana v Callais decision last month, which essentially gutted the Voting Rights Act and severely limited protections against voting discrimination. Organize

stories.title: Poll: Israel is dividing US Republicans, too
stories.summary: The Republican Party is starting to splinter over support for Israel — and President Donald Trump’s most loyal supporters are largely aligned with the embattled U.S. ally. New  results from The POLITICO Poll  find that self-identified “MAGA” Trump voters are more supportive of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s government and its relationship with the U.S. than those who don’t identify as MAGA but still voted for the president. Nearly half of MAGA Trump voters say they back Israel and

stories.title: Eurovision avoids its nightmare scenario as Bulgaria beats Israel to win
stories.summary: VIENNA — Bulgaria won the 2026 Eurovision Song Contest on Saturday, beating Israel to victory on a dramatic night in Vienna that was marred by several countries boycotting. Bulgaria won with 516 points, and will now host the competition in 2027. DARA, who represented Bulgaria with the upbeat “Bangaranga,” topped both the jury and public votes. Israel’s Noam Bettan, performing “Michelle,” came second with 343 points, in a nail-biting finish. The 70th edition of the i

stories.title: Aukus nuclear submarine base would ‘place a massive target on our backs’, NSW labour union warns
stories.summary: Outcry follows release of previously secret documents naming Port Kembla as preferred east coast Aukus base As Aukus spending and delays blow out, will Australia’s nuclear submarines ever materialise? Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A labour union has expressed alarm about newly released documents that reveal Port Kembla to be a preferred Australian base for nuclear-powered submarines, saying it would “place a ma

stories.title: ‘Fiercely loyal, endlessly generous’ father named as victim of Rottnest Island shark attack
stories.summary: Steven Mattaboni, 38, was a ‘one-of-a-kind gentleman’ and devoted parent to two young daughters, his wife Shirene says Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A father of two young daughters, husband and avid fisher has been identified as the victim of a fatal shark attack at Rottnest Island in Western Australia. Steven Mattaboni, 38, a surveyor from Perth, was in the water at Horseshoe Reef about 9.55am on Saturday when he was believed to have been bitten by a shark, police

stories.title: ‘It was like a mosh pit’: Swatch closes stores as watch launch causes crowding and scuffles
stories.summary: French police fire teargas and UK shops close for safety reasons as hundreds queue for Royal Pop timepieces that make up Audemars Piguet collaboration The launch of limited-edition Swatch watches descended into chaos in several European cities and New York, with French police firing teargas to restore order at a store near Paris. Hundreds of people waited through the night from Friday into Saturday – and in some cases for several days – hoping to buy the Royal Pop timepieces, made in collaborati

stories.title: Culture secretary says Streeting’s call for UK to rejoin EU is ‘odd’
stories.summary: Lisa Nandy told the BBC she did not understand ‘sudden focus on Europe’ from Labour leadership hopeful UK politics live – latest updates The UK culture secretary has dismissed calls for Britain to re-enter the European Union as “odd”, a day after her former cabinet colleague Wes Streeting said the country’s future lies back in the bloc . Lisa Nandy criticised the former health secretary on Sunday, saying his comments over the weekend risked reopening a debate that was settled with the Brexit ref

stories.title: Thames Water investors say temporary nationalisation would slow its recovery
stories.summary: Comments come after Andy Burnham says he would renationalise water and other businesses as PM Investors in Thames Water have told the Labour government that a temporary nationalisation of the embattled company would slow its turnaround, after calls from Andy Burnham to put key utilities under public control . As Keir Starmer’s grip on power appeared to be fading, the Greater Manchester mayor suggested at the weekend that the renationalisation of water and energy would form part of his policy age

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill six in southern Lebanon hours after extension of ceasefire
stories.summary: Three paramedics at health centre among dead, while Hamas military chief Izz al-Din al-Haddad killed in Gaza strike Israel carried out airstrikes in southern Lebanon, killing at least six people, including three paramedics working at a health centre, just hours after its envoys had agreed with the Lebanese government to extend a ceasefire . Israel also said it had killed the Hamas military chief, Izz al-Din al-Haddad, in a targeted strike in Gaza on Friday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Escaped tiger injures one near Leipzig — report
stories.summary: A tiger that was on the loose near the eastern city of Leipzig has injured at least one person, according to media reports. Police said they shot the animal.

stories.title: Middle East: Drone strike sparks fire at UAE nuclear plant
stories.summary: No party has claimed responsibility for the strike on the Barakah nuclear power plant, but Iran has repeatedly launched attacks on the UAE since the start of the war. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: China says preliminary agreement reached with US to lower some tariffs
stories.summary: China’s commerce ministry said on Saturday that Beijing and Washington had struck a preliminary agreement to reduce some tariffs, and also confirmed agricultural and aircraft deals reached during U.S. President Donald Trump’s trip to Beijing. The ministry’s statement came the day after Trump concluded a state visit to China for talks with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Trump had indicated on Friday that tariffs were not discussed in his meetings with Xi. In a statement posted on its w

stories.title: WHO declares global health emergency over Ebola outbreak
stories.summary: The World Health Organization has declared a global health emergency over the Ebola outbreak that has killed a suspected 80 people in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Neighboring countries sharing land borders with the DRC are at “high risk for further spread,” the WHO warned . The outbreak involves the Bundibugyo virus, which is harder to treat and for which there is no vaccine. There were 246 suspected cases in the DRC as of May 16, the WHO said. Uganda has reported two lab-confir

stories.title: Mali’s forces target rebel alliance in junta’s fight to keep power
stories.summary: Army supported by Russian mercenaries launches airstrikes after offensive by coalition of Islamist extremists and Tuareg separatists Mali’s armed forces, supported by Russian mercenaries, have launched airstrikes targeting a rebel alliance of Islamist extremists and Tuareg separatists as the ruling junta struggles to maintain its hold on power in the unstable west African country. Earlier this week warplanes targeted the key northern town of Kidal, which was lost when the rebels launched a surpr

stories.title: ‘A place for everybody’: Stockholm to open its first publicly run sauna
stories.summary: Unlike its Nordic neighbours, finding a place in the Swedish capital to bada bastu is hard, with years-long waiting lists at member clubs There is little doubt that Stockholm is a city of sauna-goers. All year round, from early morning to late into the night, the city’s residents can be seen emerging from wooden huts, a trail of woodsmoke coming from the chimney, and lowering themselves into the deep brackish waters of the Swedish capital’s shoreline. But, for locals and visitors alike, getting

stories.title: ‘Feels like an illusion’: inside post-Maduro Venezuela’s bewildering new era
stories.summary: Four months after Trump’s surprise raid, a political thaw has descended – but mingled with hope is trepidation for what comes next When Ángel Linares heard a strange buzz followed by an explosion, his first thought was that neighbours were setting off fireworks to celebrate the new year. Then his windows shattered, the building’s walls shook and its facade was ripped off, sending him flying on to the ground of an apartment suddenly reduced to rubble. His 85-year-old mother, Jesucita, feared Vene

stories.title: Passenger banned from Qantas after allegedly biting attendant on flight from Australia to US
stories.summary: Flight from Melbourne to Dallas forced to land in Tahiti due to man’s alleged disruptive behaviour Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A passenger has been banned from future Qantas travel after a plane travelling on a long-haul flight from Australia to the US was diverted at the weekend after the man allegedly bit a flight attendant. The QF21 flight left Melbourne at 2.30pm on Friday en route to Dallas and was diver

stories.title: Two high-profile Liberals defect to One Nation – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Jim Chalmers says the opposition’s policy to index tax brackets would cost the budget a quarter of a trillion dollars over 10 years, while claiming that the government is returning bracket creep to taxpayers. It’s a slightly tricky position, as Angus Taylor argues that Labor is “betraying” itself because the high figure is actually how much the government is keeping and spending through bracket creep ever year.

stories.title: Fire and ‘sheer volume’: how Britain’s 6m-vape problem is putting recycling under strain
stories.summary: Despite the ban on disposables, waste professionals say the mountain of discarded devices is a £1bn-a-year issue It is 2pm and Ana, 47, has just started the afternoon shift at the Suez recycling plant near Birmingham city centre, standing beneath a sign reading “Non-ferrous sorting station” with a bucket of vapes in front of her. Sorting and dismantling them is part of her job as a site operative. Recycling them is not simple. Each bucket holds between 40 and 50 devices, and over the course of a

stories.title: Andalucíans vote in election seen as gauge of Spain’s wider political change
stories.summary: Conservatives expected to keep majority as socialists face drubbing and ballot tests trajectory of far-right Vox party Voters in the southern Spanish region of Andalucía are casting their ballots in an election that is likely to deliver an absolute majority to the conservative People’s party (PP) and inflict another debilitating defeat on Pedro Sánchez’s embattled socialists in what was previously one of their proudest strongholds. Sunday’s election in Spain’s most populous region – the last big

stories.title: Workers racing to turn reflecting pool blue for Trump may be at risk, union warns
stories.summary: Union representative concerned about safety as workers rush to finish repainting DC pool before 250th celebrations Workers renovating one of Washington DC’s most historically symbolic sites in a project ordered by Donald Trump may be risking their safety as they race to finish on time for the US’s 250th anniversary celebrations , a union monitoring the site has warned. Trade union scrutiny has focused on the reflecting pool on the US capital’s National Mall – scene of Martin Luther King’s 1963 “

stories.title: Ian McKellen ‘emotional’ as he opens County Durham theatre space
stories.summary: Actor says Ensemble 84 in Horden, employing local talent, is how a professional repertory company should be It’s a chilly spring evening in what was once a Catholic church in a left-behind County Durham pit village, and Ian McKellen admits he is feeling emotional. “This is the only company of actors in the United Kingdom and it’s in … Horden?” he says. “I’m feeling very emotional. This fulfils all my romantic dreams I’ve had ever since I discovered the joys of theatre-going and acting.” Continue

stories.title: ‘We’re not ready’: US lags on pandemic preparedness after Covid, experts say
stories.summary: Experts say slashed funding and growing misinformation are some of the greatest challenges facing public health The hantavirus outbreak, while unlikely to spark the next big pandemic, is shining a spotlight on the ways public health has deteriorated in the US: its ability to test for rare diseases, its expertise on outbreak prevention and response, its ability to battle misinformation and restore trust. “Assuming everything goes well in containing this outbreak, which I hope it does, the takeawa

stories.title: Bulgaria wins Eurovision song contest with Dara's Bangaranga – video
stories.summary: Bulgarian singer's party anthem triumphs at the grand final of the 70th Eurovision song contest in Vienna, giving the country its first win the competition. The Israeli contestant, Noam Bettan, came second in a competition clouded by protests over the country's participation Continue reading...

stories.title: Labour leadership talk ‘froth and nonsense’, says senior minister – as it happened
stories.summary: Lisa Nandy says no candidate has launched a challenge to Keir Starmer, despite ‘feverish speculation’ around Andy Burnham and Wes Streeting The Conservative leader called accusations her party could not win a general election “very silly”. Trevor Phillips, questioning Kemi Badenoch on Sky, suggested she was “reluctant to accept what the voters have said” in the local election results. Continue reading...

stories.title: Experts sound alarm as North America’s bees start swarm season unusually early
stories.summary: After record losses last year, beekeepers report a warm winter has led to bees ‘waking up earlier’ this year After a series of record-breaking US heatwaves , the 2026 bee swarm season in North America has started 17 days earlier than last year, pushing beekeepers to adapt to a rapidly shifting season while raising new questions about how honeybees are responding to the climate crisis. According to a new report published by Swarmed, a tracking network of more than 10,000 beekeepers, focused on sa

stories.title: Shell-shocked: California man arrested for attempting to traffic wild turtles
stories.summary: Donald Do allegedly sought to export 292 loggerhead musk turtles under the false claim they had been captive-bred A California man, who received a federal permit to export turtles under the false claim that they had been captive-bred, has been arrested on wildlife-trafficking charges, authorities said on Friday. Donald Do and an unidentified accomplice allegedly sought to export 292 loggerhead musk turtles to Taiwan from December 2022 to May 2024. The accomplice obtained the US Fish and Wildlife

stories.title: Venezuela deports Maduro ally Alex Saab to US
stories.summary: Alex Saab fell out of favor with the new administration that took power since Maduro’s ouster and capture by US forces in January.

stories.title: WHO declares international emergency over Ebola outbreak
stories.summary: The World Health Organization said the Ebola outbreak could be much larger than what is currently being detected and reported. It warned of health risks to other countries.

stories.title: India news: CNG gas price hiked in Delhi NCR amid Iran war fallout
stories.summary: The price of CNG has increased in Delhi and the surrounding region for the second time in two days, as India grapples with the economic impact of the Iran war. Meanwhile, Modi is set to arrive in Sweden. DW has more.

stories.title: Dead humpback whale off Denmark is 'Timmy'
stories.summary: Divers have confirmed that a dead humpback whale spotted off Denmark is the same creature that spent weeks beached off Germany's Baltic coast. It comes two weeks after a contentious rescue operation.

stories.title: Germany news: UNICEF says child well-being ranking 'alarming'
stories.summary: A new study ranks Germany near the bottom in child well-being and education. Meanwhile, authorities say a dead humpback spotted off Denmark is the same creature that spent weeks beached off Germany's coast. DW has more.

stories.title: Two Nepali climbers break own Everest records
stories.summary: Two veteran Nepali climbers, a man and a woman, have each set new records for ascents of the world's highest mountain. The 492 Everest climbing permits issued by Nepal for the 2026 spring season represent another high.

stories.title: Ukrainian drones hit Russia in massive overnight attack
stories.summary: Ukraine has launched almost 600 drones at Russia in one of its largest overnight attacks amid Moscow's invasion. The region around the Russian capital was one of those targeted.

stories.title: UK far-right group stages anti-immigration protest in London
stories.summary: Far-right activist Tommy Robinson gathered thousands in London, urging them to "get involved" or "lose their country forever."

stories.title: Lights, camera, Bangaranga: Highlights from Eurovision
stories.summary: Bulgarian singer Dara stormed to victory in the contest, while Ukraine broke the record for the longest note ever sung at Eurovision.

stories.title: 'Look Mum, one point': Why does the UK keep getting Eurovision wrong?
stories.summary: After four years of flops, the BBC has to think hard about how it approaches Eurovision in 2027.

stories.title: Ebola outbreak is a global health emergency, WHO says
stories.summary: The World Health Organization said that it could be a "much larger" outbreak than what is currently being identified and reported but that is does not meet the criteria of a pandemic emergency.

stories.title: Man drives car into pedestrians in Italy, injuring eight
stories.summary: Eight people were injured, four seriously, before passers-by gave chase and stopped the man.

stories.title: Canadian from hantavirus-hit cruise ship tests positive
stories.summary: The individual is one of four former passengers on the MV Hondius isolating on Vancouver Island, British Columbia.

stories.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DR Congo a global health emergency
stories.summary: The agency added that the outbreak, with around 246 cases and 80 deaths, does not meet the criteria of pandemic emergency.

stories.title: Lebanon says six killed in Israeli strike as US announces ceasefire extension
stories.summary: President Trump announced the truce last month, but Israel and Hezbollah have continued to exchange fire since then.

stories.title: Hezbollah drone strike videos show evolving tactics against Israel
stories.summary: BBC Verify has examined dozens of videos of fibre-optic drone attacks carried out by the Lebanese armed group.

stories.title: Colorado governor commuted Tina Peters’ sentence after Trump blocked funding for clean water project, Lauren Boebert claims – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. Colorado governor commutes sentence of election denier Tina Peters Sign up for the Breaking News US email Jamieson Greer also said US export controls on semiconductor chips were not a major topic of discussions with Chinese officials in Beijing . The US trade representative’s comments to Bloomberg on Friday suggest a breakthrough on selling Nvidia’s advanced H200 chips to China remains far away, Reuters is reporting, despite Nvidia chief Jensen Huang ’s last-minute

stories.title: Seven people injured after man drives car into pedestrians in northern Italy
stories.summary: Man in 30s arrested over incident in Modena that left two seriously hurt Eight people were injured, two seriously, on Saturday after a car rammed into a group of pedestrians in the northern Italian city of Modena. Police said the driver, in his 30s, had been arrested. He is also alleged to have attempted to stab a passerby who had tried to stop him from fleeing the scene. Continue reading...

stories.title: Canada confirms first hantavirus case in isolation in British Columbia
stories.summary: The person was on board the MV Hondius, the center of the outbreak that has claimed three lives Canadian officials said on Saturday that a test for one of the four Canadians currently quarantining in British Columbia after being exposed to the hantavirus while on board the cruise ship where the outbreak occurred indicated a positive result. Speaking at a news conference , Dr Bonnie Henry, British Columbia’s provincial health officer, said the individual developed mild symptoms, including fever a

stories.title: Study: Immigrants disadvantaged in German housing market
stories.summary: The German housing market crisis is deepening social inequality. Newcomers in particular are struggling to find affordable housing — with consequences for integration, education, and the labor market.

stories.title: Thailand: Bangkok rail crossing collision kills at least 8
stories.summary: A collision between a freight train and a bus occurred in downtown Bangkok. Fatal road accidents are not uncommon in Thailand.

stories.title: Germany gets ahead in the new space race
stories.summary: German companies of all sizes and all over the country are developing satellites and space applications. They want to be part of the lucrative industry, which is increasingly crucial for civilian and military purposes.

stories.title: Eight injured after car ploughs into crowd in Italy's Modena
stories.summary: Investigators are still determining if the driver acted deliberately. Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni called the incident in Modena "extremely serious."

stories.title: Rwandan genocide suspect Felicien Kabuga dies in custody
stories.summary: Rwanda's Felicien Kabuga was once one of the world's most-wanted fugitives. He was accused of encouraging and bankrolling the 1994 genocide.

stories.title: Freight train and bus crash kills at least eight in Bangkok
stories.summary: Police and rescue officials said the collision sparked a fire that engulfed the bus.

stories.title: Rescue diver dies during search for bodies of Italians who drowned in Maldives caves
stories.summary: Staff Sgt Mohamed Mahdhee died while diving in the search-and-rescue operation, the Maldives government tells the BBC.

stories.title: Toddlers among more than 50 schoolchildren kidnapped in Nigeria
stories.summary: No group has yet claimed responsibility for the attacks, which have devastated Mussa town in Borno state.

stories.title: London on edge as Tommy Robinson backers and pro-Palestinian marchers rally
stories.summary: Tens of thousands of people joined rival London marches for Tommy Robinson and Palestine, as 4,000 officers used strict routes to avoid clashes and keep order.

stories.title: Whale found dead near Danish island after German rescue operation
stories.summary: Wildlife experts had previous criticised the operation, saying it would only cause the animal more distress.

stories.title: Taiwan insists it is independent after Trump warning
stories.summary: Fresh from his summit in Beijing, the US president warned against a formal declaration of independence by the island.

stories.title: South Korea: Thousands watch robot monks march through Seoul’s historic streets
stories.summary: Four robot monks in traditional robes joined Seoul’s lantern parade ahead of Buddha’s birthday, symbolising Buddhism’s tech-savvy modernisation.

stories.title: Eurovision final in Vienna overshadowed by protests over Israel’s participation
stories.summary: Hundreds protested in Vienna against Israel’s participation in Eurovision 2026 as security was tightened around the Wiener Stadthalle before the grand final, amid tensions linked to the war in Gaza.

stories.title: Foreign tourists are falling out of love with Goa - here's why
stories.summary: Foreign visitors to Goa have nearly halved from their pre-Covid peak even as domestic tourism is booming.

stories.title: The girl group who've sold out venues and toured the world - without releasing a record
stories.summary: The all-female supergroup has sold out venues across the UK and Ireland, toured stadiums with Ed Sheeran, and built a global following - all without releasing a debut record.

stories.title: Secret police and propaganda: The Chinese agents spying on expats in US
stories.summary: Experts say two espionage cases in the US highlight China's attempts to peddle influence globally over the past decade.

stories.title: Itt az ideje megteremteni az avarégetés-mentes országot
stories.summary: Ősszel és tavasszal a vidéki kertekben menetrendszerűen beindulnak a füstgyárak, ahol a lehulló falevelektől és nyesedékektől így próbálnak megszabadulni a kerttulajdonosok. Mióta és miért égetünk egyáltalán, van-e bármi haszna ennek a tevékenységnek, vagy minden további nélkül egyik napról a másikra megszüntethető lenne?

stories.title: Liz Nugent: Sally Diamond, a furcsa lány (részlet)
stories.summary: Egy világvégi ír falucska magányos kis házikója hirtelen az érdeklődés középpontjába kerül, mikor egyetlen lakójáról, a negyvenkét éves, teljes elzártságban élő Sally Diamondról kiderül, hogy halála után elégette édesapját. A dolog még nagyobb vihart kavar, mikor lassan kirajzolódik Sally traumatikus múltja is, és bár sokan segítenek neki visszaszerezni a kontrollt az élete fölött, egy nap rejtélyes csomagja érkezik egy ismeretlentől Új-Zélandról, és ez mindent megváltoztat. Liz Nugent feszültsé

stories.title: A NER-nek vége, a kritikus színháznak nem – mi történik a rendszerváltás után a színpadon?
stories.summary: A színház feladata, hogy érzékenyen reagáljon az aktuális társadalmi és közéleti folyamatokra, és kritikusan viszonyuljon a közeghez, amelyben létezik. De mi történik akkor, amikor egyik napról a másikra megszűnik a rendszer, amit kritizált? NER utáni színházkörkép.

stories.title: Az utolsó célpont is bemérve
stories.summary: Ethan Hawk sorozatgyilkosa nem képes meghalni, a brit tévézés nagyágyúi újra egymásnak feszülnek, Jack Ryan pedig nagyon nem bírja a nyugdíjas éveket. Heti streamingajánló.

stories.title: A fokozódó szélsőséges időjárási események nem fognak leülni tárgyalni velünk
stories.summary: A klímaváltozás egyre nagyobb és kiszámíthatatlanabb károkat okoz, miközben a biztosítási rendszer egyre nehezebben tud lépést tartani velük. A növekvő „védelmi rés” már nemcsak környezeti, hanem nemzetgazdasági problémát is jelent. A megoldás része a természet helyreállítása – ehhez a biztosítási szektor is újfajta eszközökkel járulhat hozzá.

stories.title: Hegedűs Zsolt adatokkal vázolta fel, milyen állapotban veszi át az egészségügyet
stories.summary: Az ápolóhiány meghaladja a 30 ezer főt – túlóra, kiégés, romló munkakörülmények.

stories.title: Újra él az orosz tengeri olajszállításra kivetett amerikai szankció
stories.summary: Áprilisban függesztették fel, hogy az iráni háború miatt emelkedő olajárakat mérsékeljék.

stories.title: Elzárták a vízcsapot Budakalászon a szabálytalan lakásszaporítók előtt
stories.summary: Nem kaphat vízműbekötési hozzájárulást az, aki a helyi építési szabályzattal ellentétesen, átépítéssel megszaporítja egy ingatlanon belüli lakásainak számát, egyezett meg az egyre terjedő jelenség megfékezésében a budakalászi önkormányzat és a Duna Menti Regionális Vízmű. Mindkettőnek jó oka volt az egyezségre.

stories.title: Újra drónokkal támadta Ukrajna Moszkvát, hárman meghaltak
stories.summary: Százhúsz drónt indított Ukrajna az orosz főváros felé vasárnapra virradóra.

stories.title: A vasárnap még esős lesz, hétfőtől melegszik az idő
stories.summary: A pünkösdi hosszú hétvégén nyárias idő lesz.

stories.title: Nemzetközi szintű vészhelyzetet hirdetett a WHO a Kelet-Afrikában kitört ebola-járvány miatt
stories.summary: A helyzetet súlyosbítja, hogy a járványt egy olyan ritka vírustörzs okozza, amire egyelőre nem létezik engedélyezett oltóanyag vagy gyógyszer.

stories.title: Természetfilm-premier a Telexen: néhány óráig gyönyörködünk bennük, de ennél sokkal többet is adnak nekünk
stories.summary: A látványosan rajzó kérészek lárva korukban óriási szerepet játszanak abban, hogy tiszta ivóvízhez jussunk. A lámpafényekkel és az aszfaltcsíkokkal is alaposan megvezetjük őket, derül ki a Kérészek nyomában című természetfilmből.

stories.title: Szájkarate: Trashcelebek vs. Ruszin-Szendi turbulenciái
stories.summary: Keressük a kampányidőszak legbizarrabb történetét. Melyik borított ki jobban minket: a Curtis, Jaber és a többiek vagy a volt vezérkari főnök vegzálása?

stories.title: C. Ronaldo elbukta a kupadöntőt, nem vette át az ezüstérmet
stories.summary: Csütörtökön bajnok lehet csapatával, az lenne az első szaúdi trófeája.

stories.title: ADHD-ihletett számmal Bulgária nyerte meg az idei Euróvíziós Dalfesztivált
stories.summary: A második helyet Izrael szerezte meg, pedig az ország részvétele miatt többen is bojkottálták az idei versenyt.

stories.title: Most már el tudom képzelni, hogyan lehetne jobb a Forza Horizon
stories.summary: A gyorsautós játéksorozat hatodik része Japánba megy, ahol szerencsére nagyobb a nyugi, bár nem mindig sikerülhet élvezni.

stories.title: Kutatók megfejthették, miért jobbkezes az emberek többsége
stories.summary: Kiderült, hogy valószínűleg az agyméretünknek és a két lábon járásnak is köze van ahhoz, hogy az emberiség 90 százaléka a jobb kezét preferálja.

stories.title: Újabb fideszes lap szűnik meg, elbúcsúzott olvasóitól a Győr+ Hetilap
stories.summary: „Köszönjük a bizalmat, a kritikákat, a bátorító szavakat és azt, hogy ennyi éven át velünk tartottak” – írták az utolsó nyomtatott számban.

stories.title: Gajdos László bemutatta az utódát a Nyíregyházi Állatpark élén
stories.summary: Papp Endre állatorvosként került a parkba, ez volt az első munkahelye.

stories.title: Durván elszálltak a foci vb árai, a döntőre a legolcsóbb elérhető jegy 2,4 millió forint
stories.summary: A tömegközlekedés sem marad ki a trendből, van ahol a hétszeresére emelték a vonatjegyek árát a meccsek napjaira.

stories.title: Xabi Alonso visszatér Angliába, de nem a Liverpool, hanem a Chelsea edzője lesz
stories.summary: A spanyol edzőt a télen menesztette a Real Madrid.

stories.title: A választások előtt 40 milliós NKA-támogatást kapott egy Fideszhez köthető szervezet, aminek a székhelyén csak egy romos épület áll
stories.summary: A Szabó Zsolt volt fideszes képviselőhöz köthető cégnek még honlapja sincs, az NKA-tól egy másik, vele összefüggésbe hozható hatvani alapítvány is több tízmillió forintot kapott.

stories.title: Amikor a torta sós, és ez nem baj
stories.summary: A quiche a tökéletes válasz arra a kérdésre, ha valami hordozható, látványos, de praktikus, mégis jól variálható ételre van szükség: piknikek, strandolások, tavaszi zöldségek megoldása egyben.

stories.title: A ChatGPT mostantól a bankszámlákra is rálát
stories.summary: Külön engedélyezni kell neki, és egyelőre csak Amerikában elérhető a funkció, amire a fejlesztője szerint óriási igény volt a felhasználók felől.

stories.title: Krusovszky Dénes: Mintha megint azt várnánk, hogy majd az új kormány megcsinálja nekünk a tutit
stories.summary: A jelenleg Japánban élő költő, író szerint itt az ideális pillanat a kulturális autonómia radikálisabb újragondolására, de nem hiszi, hogy ebbe az irányba fogunk ellépni. Sokkal valószínűbbnek tartja, hogy ugyanazt az államfüggő modellt fogjuk továbbgörgetni, foltozgatni, megbütykölni, amit megörököltünk. Interjú.

stories.title: Meghalt egy mentőbúvár a Maldív-szigeteken, aki az olasz áldozatok keresésére indult
stories.summary: Öt olasz állampolgár halt meg a pénteki balesetben a Vaavu-atollon.

stories.title: Harminckettedszerre mászta meg a Mount Everestet, újabb világrekordot állított fel a nepáli serpa
stories.summary: Az 56 éves Kami Rita először 1994-ben mászta meg az Everestet, azóta majdnem minden évben elérte a csúcsot.

stories.title: Még a testvéri szeretetnek is határok kellenek
stories.summary: A Szarvasbébivel berobbanó Richard Gadd Fivérség című sorozata hat órányi tömény szenvedés és fájdalom, mégis ott tart a képernyő előtt.

stories.title: Szalay-Bobrovniczky Kristóf szerint évek óta készítették elő a 4ig 1311 milliárd forintos szerződését
stories.summary: Az összegről azt mondta: a hadiiparban sajnos ilyen számok vannak.

stories.title: Dróncsapás ért egy atomerőművet az Egyesült Arab Emírségekben
stories.summary: Az első jelentések szerint csak kisebb tüzet okozott, senki nem sérült meg, a sugárzási szint normális.

stories.title: Threatened indictment of Raúl Castro ratchets up US pressure on Cuba
stories.summary: Trump administration move echoes indictment of Venezuela’s Nicolás Maduro as fuel crisis racks Cuba Tensions between Cuba and US seem set to rise further amid reports that Raúl Castro, the country’s 94-year-old former president, may soon face the type of indictment that led to the US abduction of the Venezuelan leader, Nicolás Maduro, in January. Although Raúl is officially retired, he remains the most potent figure in Cuban politics following the death of his brother Fidel in 2016, and by targe

stories.title: Ebola outbreak kills 65 people in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo
stories.summary: Uganda also reports outbreak and health officials say cases were caused by Bundibugyo strain of virus An outbreak of Ebola has killed 65 people in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, health officials said. There have been 246 suspected cases of the haemorrhagic fever reported so far in the conflict-hit Ituri province, which shares borders with Uganda and South Sudan. Continue reading...

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Russian drone attacks on Kyiv, Israeli strikes in Lebanon, Trump in Beijing and a mural of Lamine Yamal – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Continue reading...

stories.title: Donald Trump does ‘not feel optimistic’ for Jimmy Lai after speaking with Xi Jinping
stories.summary: Family and supporters had hoped the US president could help free the 78-year-old British citizen during summit talks in Beijing Donald Trump raised the case of jailed Hong Kong democracy campaigner Jimmy Lai in talks with Chinese leader Xi Jinping but was told it “is a tough one”. Family and supporters of the 78-year-old British citizen had hoped the US president could help secure his release. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump says Islamic State ‘second in command’ killed by US and Nigerian forces
stories.summary: US president calls Abu-Bilal al-Minuki ‘most active terrorist in the world’ and says he was eliminated in ‘very complex mission’ Donald Trump has said US and Nigerian forces killed the “second in command” global leader of the Islamic State. “Tonight, at my direction, brave American forces and the Armed Forces of Nigeria flawlessly executed a meticulously planned and very complex mission to eliminate the most active terrorist in the world from the battlefield,” the US president said on his Truth

stories.title: Israel confirms Hamas armed wing chief killed in Gaza strike
stories.summary: The military said Haddad was “among the last senior commanders in Hamas’s military wing responsible for planning and carrying out the October 7th massacre.”

stories.title: US reportedly dropped fraud charges against Indian billionaire after he hired Trump’s lawyer
stories.summary: Gautam Adani, richest man in Asia, was accused of conspiring to pay $250m in bribes to Indian government officials The US Department of Justice is dropping its fraud charges against the Indian billionaire Gautam Adani, the richest man in Asia, after he hired a new legal team led by Donald Trump’s personal lawyer, according to new reports. In an undisclosed April meeting at the justice department, Trump’s personal lawyer, Robert J Giuffra Jr, said that Adani would invest $10bn in the US economy a

stories.title: After four bloody years, the war on Ukraine might be turning into Putin’s undoing | Rajan Menon
stories.summary: A battered economy, huge numbers of casualties and very little territorial gain – it’s no wonder even stalwart Putin supporters are showing signs of disquiet Rajan Menon is professor emeritus of international relations at Powell School, City University of New York On 9 May, Russia held its iconic annual Victory Day parade to honour the sacrifices of its soldiers and civilians during its four-year war against Nazi Germany. When the president, Vladimir Putin, invaded Ukraine on 24 February 2022, h

stories.title: German intelligence offices snub US-based Palantir software
stories.summary: Germany's domestic intelligence agency has reportedly chosen a data analysis system from France, instead of US-based Palantir. Civil society has welcomed the move, but says more must be done.

stories.title: North Korea slams UK over sanctions on camp allegedly linked to abducted Ukrainian children
stories.summary: Since the war in Ukraine erupted in 2022, 2,318 Ukrainian children remain missing, while 20,570 have been deported or forcibly displaced and 704 killed, according to a Ukrainian government portal.

stories.title: Oman caught between US and Iran after Tehran’s claims of joint strait of Hormuz plan
stories.summary: Muscat silent about plans – opposed by US – to charge fee and demand details on nationality of all transiting ships Oman has been caught in geopolitical crossfire after Iran said it was coordinating with the Gulf state over the future management of the strait of Hormuz, including Tehran’s plans to impose fees on commercial shipping. The Omani exclave of Musandam lies to the south of the contested waterway, which normally carries a fifth of the world’s seaborne oil traffic but has been blockaded

stories.title: ‘Extremely cruel and tragic’: Iranian director Asghar Farhadi speaks out against state violence and the war
stories.summary: The film-maker, who won the Grand Prix for A Hero in 2021, condemned both the killing of protesters and the conflict’s bombing campaigns during a Cannes press conference Oscar-winning Iranian director Asghar Farhadi has described the deaths of civilians in Iran as “extremely cruel and tragic” during a press conference at the Cannes film festival. Farhadi, whose new Paris-set drama Parallel Tales premiered on the Croisette on Thursday night, was asked about working free from censorship in France,

stories.title: Shaky truce between Israel and Lebanon extended for 45 days, US says
stories.summary: Statement following ‘productive’ talks in Washington comes as Israel launches strikes on southern city of Tyre Israel and Lebanon have agreed to a 45-day extension of their ceasefire after another round of talks in Washington, the US state department has said. It came after two “productive” days of talks, and more negotiations would be held from 2-3 June, the department spokesperson Tommy Pigott said. Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump’s lack of focus on human rights in China is big departure for US diplomacy
stories.summary: Change reflects both transformation of US in Trump era and China’s increasing confidence on world stage Asked before he departed for Beijing if he would raise with the Chinese president the case of Jimmy Lai , the pro-democracy activist jailed in Hong Kong, Donald Trump said: “I’ll bring him up.” But, the US president added: “It’s like saying to me, ‘If Comey ever went to jail, would you let him out?’ It might be a hard one for me.” Trump was referring to James B Comey, a former FBI director and

stories.title: Arrest of Iraqi terror suspect with alleged links to Iran’s Quds Force is astonishing but not surprising
stories.summary: Iran has a long history of unconventional operations, all designed to divert, distract and destabilise current or potential enemies The arrest by US authorities of an alleged Iraqi commander of an Iranian-backed militia group now accused of responsibility for 18 terrorist attacks in the UK, Europe and Canada since the beginning of the Iran war is an astonishing development – yet not the least bit surprising. According to a complaint unsealed on Friday in a federal court in Manhattan, Mohammad Ba

stories.title: Búcsúzóul közel 50 milliárdos szerződést kötött Rogán Antal minisztériuma Balásy Gyula cégeivel
stories.summary: Összesen nyolc szerződést kötött 2025 decembere és 2026 márciusa között a Rogán Antal vezette Miniszterelnöki Kabinetiroda Balásy Gyula kommunikációs cégeivel.

stories.title: Áfra János: Az ózdi bölény ébredése
stories.summary: „A titokzatos történetek, felfedezésre váró állatok egyre jobban érdekelték, az emberek pedig egyre kevésbé.” E heti novellánk főszereplője egy biztonsági őr, akinek különös, az egész valóságérzékelésére kiható hobbija van.

stories.title: Miután a Fidesz nekiment a Norvég Civil Alapnak, L. Ritók Nórát felkeresték az Alkotmányvédelmi Hivatal tisztviselői
stories.summary: „Minden nemzetbiztonsági eszköz a rendelkezésükre áll, hogy kövessék a munkáját” – mondták neki a tisztviselők az első találkozáskor, majd egy éven keresztül rendszeresen keresték.

stories.title: Autó hajtott a gyalogosok közé Modena belvárosában, nyolcan megsérültek
stories.summary: Massimo Mezzetti polgármester szerint négy sérült állapota kritikus, egyikük, egy kirakatnak szorult nő akár mindkét lábát is elveszítheti.

stories.title: UK politics: How Starmer survives as Farage waits in wings
stories.summary: High profile resignations, a prime minister on the edge and populists on the up — UK politics is far from dull. DW explains what's going on in Keir Starmer's Labour party and with Nigel Farage. And what may be next.

stories.title: Ukraine: Dozens of European countries sign up to special tribunal plan to prosecute Russia
stories.summary: At talks in Moldova, more than 30 countries have said they will join a future special tribunal for Ukraine prosecuting Russia over its invasion. How the body will force Russians to stand trial is not clear.

stories.title: Islamic feminism more widespread in Bosnia and Herzegovina
stories.summary: Many Bosnian Muslim women want to attend mosque on Fridays and to play a greater role in decision-making. Slowly the official structures of the Islamic Community are changing.

stories.title: Germany news: Chancellor Merz says he wouldn't advise his kids to go to the US
stories.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz said he wouldn't currently advise young Germans like his children to move to the US to study or work. He cited "the social climate that has suddenly developed."

stories.title: 'From collection to connection': How museums are redefining themselves
stories.summary: Gone are the days when museums were just places to see art and artifacts. On International Museum Day, DW looks at how museums are becoming unique spaces of community engagement and participation.

stories.title: Five Italians die in Maldives cave diving tragedy
stories.summary: Rescue teams in the Maldives continued recovery operations after the country’s deadliest diving accident killed five Italian divers.

stories.title: What is the 'Thucydides Trap' that Xi invoked in his meeting with Trump?
stories.summary: Xi Jinping opened his Beijing summit with Trump by asking whether the two powers could avoid the "Thucydides Trap" — then warned that mishandling Taiwan could lead to war.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon extend their ceasefire by 45 days following talks in the US
stories.summary: The US State Department will hold negotiations aimed at reaching a permanent political agreement on the 2 and 3 June. Beforehand, the Pentagon is to bring together military delegations from the countries concerned on 29 May.

stories.title: What China critics in Maga movement make of Trump's Beijing trip
stories.summary: Trump’s softer approach towards China is one that experts say will likely trickle down to the wider MAGA base.

stories.title: Switzerland to open secret files on Auschwitz 'Angel of Death' Mengele
stories.summary: Mengele fled Europe after the war but for years there were rumours he spent time in Switzerland.

stories.title: US planning to charge ex-Cuban leader Raúl Castro
stories.summary: Charges could come as soon as next week in a case reportedly focused on Cuba's downing of two planes in 1996.

stories.title: A call to stop the global housing crisis: World Urban Forum, why it matters?
stories.summary: More than 40,000 participants are set to gather in Baku for the UN's World Urban Forum as UN-Habitat warns that 3 billion people lack adequate housing and slum populations could triple by 2050.

stories.title: Trump says 'most active terrorist in the world' killed in US-Nigerian operation
stories.summary: The "meticulously planned and very complex mission" eliminated Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, the Islamic State group's second in command globally, Trump said.

stories.title: Trump's independence warning prompts response from Taiwan
stories.summary: Taiwan's foreign ministry said in a statement following Trump's comments that "Beijing has no right to claim jurisdiction over Taiwan."

stories.title: Putin to visit China days after Trump's Beijing trip, Kremlin says
stories.summary: Putin and Chinese President Xi Jinping will discuss ways to deepen their countries' "comprehensive partnership and strategic cooperation," the Kremlin said.

stories.title: Senior IS leader killed in joint operation, US and Nigeria say
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said the operation eliminated "the most active terrorist in the world", Abu-Bilal al-Minuki.

stories.title: Judge orders Trump administration to return Colombian woman deported to DRC back to the US
stories.summary: Judge called Adriana Maria Quiroz Zapata’s deportation to the Democratic Republic of Congo ‘likely illegal’ A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to bring a Colombian woman back to the US from the Democratic Republic of Congo , after she was deported to the African country that had refused to accept her. The deportation of Adriana María Quiroz Zapata “was likely illegal”, the US district judge Richard Leon ruled on Wednesday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Will Ukraine corruption scandal reach Volodymyr Zelenskyy?
stories.summary: A drawn-out corruption affair in Ukraine has widened, with authorities now taking action against Andriy Yermak, former head of the presidential office. Will the scandal reach all the way to President Volodymyr Zelenskyy?

stories.title: Foreign student fee hikes in France trigger backlash
stories.summary: A French government plan to charge students from outside the European Union 16 times more than previously has stoked discontent. But how does this compare to the rest of Europe, both inside the EU and out?

stories.title: US reportedly seeks to indict Cuba's ex-president Raúl Castro as energy crisis deepens
stories.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio has renewed an offer of €85 million in aid on the condition that the assistance be distributed by the Catholic Church, bypassing the government.

stories.title: Protests erupt in Havana as Cuba faces severe fuel shortages
stories.summary: Small protests broke out in Havana over worsening shortages of food, power, and fuel, as Cuban authorities blamed US sanctions for the crisis and warned that fuel reserves had run out.

stories.title: Death toll from large-scale Russian attack that slammed Ukraine rises to 24
stories.summary: The drone and missile barrage, that mostly targeted Kyiv, marked one of Russia's largest attacks in the past four years. Many EU leaders voiced support for Ukraine.

stories.title: Funeral in Jabalia after Israeli strike hits Gaza wake despite ceasefire
stories.summary: Residents of Jabalia bury two Palestinians killed in an Israeli strike on a wake, as Gaza reports nearly 73,000 dead and truce violations persist.

stories.title: Delta Goodrem Eclipse Eurovision second semi-final performance in full — video
stories.summary: Australia’s 2026 Eurovision hopeful Delta Goodrem delivers a powerful performance on the 70th anniversary of the global song contest, becoming the first Australian act to qualify for the grand final since 2023. Heading into the competition as an early favourite behind Eurovision heavy hitters Denmark and Finland, Goodrem delivers a note-perfect rendition of her power-ballad entry, Eclipse. Her performance marks the 11th time Australia has appeared in the multinational song contest Eurovision 202

stories.title: Weather tracker: Furnace Creek sizzles as snow sweeps Siberia in a week of extremes
stories.summary: Record heat in North and Central America coincides with egg-sized hailstones in eastern China Extreme weather across several parts of the world this week has brought record-breaking temperatures to Honduras, North America and Indonesia. Honduras smashed its all-time May maximum temperature record earlier this month – only for it to be broken again on 13 May in Choluteca, known as the furnace of Central America. Temperatures climbed to 42.2C (107.9F), surpassing the previous record of 42.1C. With

stories.title: UAE to complete second oil pipeline bypassing strait of Hormuz by 2027
stories.summary: State oil company fast-tracks previously undisclosed project, which is expected to double export capacity Business live – latest updates The United Arab Emirates has announced it will complete a new oil pipeline bypassing the strait of Hormuz by next year to secure its future crude exports against the threat of disruption. The current blockade of the vital waterway, through which 20% of oil and seaborne gas flowed before the Iran war , is approaching the 11-week mark, sending energy prices soari

stories.title: Who is Mario Draghi, this year's Charlemagne Prize winner?
stories.summary: The Charlemagne Prize honors those who advance European unity. This year's winner is Italy's Mario Draghi, a former European Central Bank chief and a key architect of European stability during a time of economic crisis.

stories.title: Lab-grown Tyrannosaurus leather: More chicken than dinosaur?
stories.summary: A handbag is promoted as the "world's first T. rex leather product". But scientists are questioning its authenticity.

stories.title: UK: Health Secretary Wes Streeting resigns, likely in bid to topple PM Keir Starmer
stories.summary: Two Labour Party politicians have positioned themselves to challenge Prime Minister Keir Starmer for the leadership. Health Secretary Wes Streeting resigned, and Angela Rayner said her tax affairs were back in order.

stories.title: Asteroid 2026 JH2 — no need to worry about it hitting Earth
stories.summary: This big asteroid was discovered less than a fortnight before its flyby Earth. But any time you see a headline about asteroids flying by the planet, you can relax. Big and small asteroids fly by Earth all the time.

stories.title: "Super Mario" celebrated for defending the euro and European unity
stories.summary: Former European Central Bank president Mario Draghi received the 2026 Charlemagne Prize on Thursday, 14 May, in Aachen, Germany, in recognition of his contributions to European unity and his leadership during the eurozone debt crisis of the 2010s.

stories.title: Hundreds of illegal motorbikes bulldozed in New York City crime crackdown
stories.summary: A moped had been used in the deadly stray-bullet shooting of a 7-month-old in Brooklyn last month, officials said.

stories.title: Erdoğan visits Astana as Turkey and Central Asia deepen ties amid shifting regional order
stories.summary: Turkey and Kazakhstan signed a friendship and strategic partnership declaration in Astana, targeting €13 billion in trade.

stories.title: BBC on the ground during march through Jerusalem's Old City
stories.summary: Tens of thousands of Israeli celebrants have marched through the area captured by Israel in the 1967 Arab-Israeli war.

stories.title: Trump and Xi wrap up summit claiming progress in stabilising ties but differences remain
stories.summary: The two leaders met on Friday at final talks of the superpower summit, which the US president said "settled a lot of different problems". Xi described Trump's visit as a "milestone visit".

stories.title: Exclusive: Inside Gaza's hunger crisis as aid falters and funding dries up
stories.summary: Community kitchens serving basic foodstuffs like lentils once a week are the last food source for hundreds of thousands in Gaza, as aid flows have dropped by 37% and only 10% of funding has been secured, Euronews learns on the ground in the Strip.

stories.title: Iran eyes fees on Hormuz internet cables as new lever against the West
stories.summary: IRGC-linked media have called on Tehran to impose fees on submarine cables through the Strait of Hormuz and monitor global data traffic, citing claimed sovereignty over the waterway.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon meet for third round of direct talks in Washington
stories.summary: Israel and Lebanon will meet for a second day in Washington to continue its third round of direct talks, as fighting between Israel and Hezbollah continues.

stories.title: Xi gives Trump private tour of secret garden in Beijing
stories.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping accompanies US counterpart Donald Trump on a walk through the gardens of Zhongnanhai, a central leadership compound next to Beijing's Forbidden City.

stories.title: King Charles and Idris Elba share the DJ stage at royal garden party
stories.summary: King Charles III marks 50 years of The King’s Trust at Buckingham Palace, celebrating its support for 1.5 million disadvantaged young people worldwide.

stories.title: Iraq swears in new prime minister as US presses Baghdad to disarm Iran-backed groups
stories.summary: Iraqi parliament approved Ali al-Zaidi's government on Thursday, with the new prime minister vowing a state monopoly on weapons as Washington leans on Baghdad to disarm Iran-backed groups.

stories.title: Underwhelming summit outcome in China brings Trump back to reality
stories.summary: After raising high expectations ahead of his trip to Beijing, the U.S president leaves with little to show for it, disappointing investors. On key flashpoints such as Iran and Taiwan, China didn't give any ground.

stories.title: Watch: Ukraine and Russia swap 205 prisoners each in major exchange deal
stories.summary: Ukraine and Russia swap 205 prisoners each in the first stage of a planned 1,000-for-1,000 exchange, mediated by the United Arab Emirates.

stories.title: Women's rights and demographics take centre stage at regional forum in Bukhara
stories.summary: Regional leaders and UN officials gathered in Bukhara to discuss how investing in women, education and demographic planning could shape long-term development across Asia.

stories.title: Watch: What did we learn from Trump's visit to China?
stories.summary: President Trump has concluded a two-day visit to Beijing at a high-stakes summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

stories.title: UN pleads for Equatorial Guinea not to send US asylum seekers to their home countries: ‘Their life would be in danger’
stories.summary: Human rights experts make rare public appeal as US deportees describe being held in ‘prison-like’ conditions Human rights experts at the United Nations issued a rare public appeal to Equatorial Guinea, urging the central African country to halt its plans to return US deportees to their home countries, where they face political violence, torture and death. The statement , co-signed by a representative of the African Commission on Human and People’s Rights, adds diplomatic pressure on Equatorial G

stories.title: The Kabul rehab centre hit by deadly Pakistani strike
stories.summary: Pakistan says it hit 'military and terrorist infrastructure' - but the UN and victims' families reject this claim.

stories.title: Angela Rayner calls blocking Andy Burnham’s return to parliament a mistake as pressure mounts on Keir Starmer – as it happened
stories.summary: Catherine West issues ultimatum for PM as ex-minister Josh Simons joins calls for prime minister to quit At the start of her programme Laura Kuenssberg addressed Catherine West and Bridget Phillipson who were sitting waiting for the main interviews. Kuenssberg told West she wanted a cabinet minister to challenge Keir Starmer. She said she was sitting next to one of them. What was her message to her? Well, there’s nothing stopping Bridget from standing. Why are all the men better than the women?

stories.title: EU preparing to offer key concession to UK in new post-Brexit agricultural deal
stories.summary: Exclusive: Britain expected to be allowed to keep ban on live animal exports, sources say, in fillip for Keir Starmer UK politics live – latest updates Brussels is preparing to offer Keir Starmer a key concession in talks over an agricultural deal, giving the beleaguered prime minister an important victory in his efforts to move closer to the EU. European officials have conceded that the UK can keep its ban on live animal exports as part of any joint deal on food and agricultural products, accor

stories.title: EU invites Taliban members to discuss Afghan migrant returns
stories.summary: The European Commission has invited officials from Afghanistan's Taliban to discuss the returns of certain migrants. It's controversial on humanitarian grounds, and because Brussels doesn't recognize Taliban authority.

stories.title: Germany's shortfall in medium-range defense capabilities
stories.summary: The US has announced that it will not be deploying Tomahawk cruise missiles in Germany. Now the German government is looking for alternatives. Could drones be part of the solution?

stories.title: Seoul court orders North Korea's leader to pay compensation to Korean War POWs
stories.summary: Thursday's ruling was the third time former South Korean POWs have won damages against Kim and North Korea, according to local reports, although none has yet succeeded in collecting compensation.

stories.title: Peace activists rally to protect Palestinians during Jerusalem flag march
stories.summary: Israeli peace activists gathered in Jerusalem on Thursday to support Palestinian residents ahead of the annual Jerusalem Day flag march, an event often marked by nationalist tensions and heavy security.

stories.title: UK: Keir Starmer tells Cabinet he will continue in place, as four junior ministers quit
stories.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has met with his Cabinet amid continued pressure after election losses. A set piece speech yesterday failed to calm the waters, and four junior ministers resigned on Tuesday.

stories.title: Hotter 2026 and El Nino could trigger extreme fires
stories.summary: More than 150 million hectares — over twice the size of Texas — burned globally in the first months of 2026. With a high chance of a supercharged El Nino, the second half of the year could be even worse.

stories.title: Why men are less worried than women about climate change
stories.summary: From petro-masculinity to electric trucks: why men disengage from climate action and what might bring them back.

stories.title: Satellites add a new layer to global poverty data
stories.summary: A new study using satellite data finds that 58% of the global population has been assigned to the wrong tier in the UN's Human Development Index. That can have real-world consequences for how aid reaches people.

stories.title: China’s new rules give the West a new headache
stories.summary: Beijing now has strong new powers to punish firms for pulling manufacturing out of China. Multinationals are increasingly caught in a web of coercion and complex rules as they try to operate across the US, EU and China.

stories.title: Ceasefire in sight? What's next for Russia's war in Ukraine
stories.summary: Four years since Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine, is the end finally in sight? DW spoke to US political experts about why they think US home elections could bring about a ceasefire.
